steps.summary:
file	README.md
dir	_build
dir	lib
file	mix.exs
dir	test

# Inventory Audit

`InventoryAudit.anomalies/3` receives three CSV-like strings:

- stock: `sku,on_hand`
- movements: `type,sku,quantity`
- counts: `sku,counted`

Movement rules:

- `shipment`, `return`, and `adjustment` add quantity.
- `sale` and `damage` subtract quantity.

Expected stock is starting on_hand plus all movements for a SKU.
Delta is counted minus expected. Only non-zero deltas are anomalies.
A positive delta has status `over`; a negative delta has status `short`.
Sort anomalies by absolute delta descending, then by SKU ascending.

`InventoryAudit.report/3` renders one line per anomaly:

`sku=<sku> expected=<expected> counted=<counted> delta=<delta> status=<status>`

defmodule InventoryAudit do
  @moduledoc false

  def anomalies(stock_csv, _movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock = parse_pairs(stock_csv)
    counts = parse_pairs(count_csv)

    stock
    |> Enum.map(fn {sku, expected} ->
      counted = Map.get(counts, sku, expected)
      delta = counted - expected

      %{
        sku: sku,
        expected: expected,
        counted: counted,
        delta: delta,
        status: if(delta > 0, do: "over", else: "ok")
      }
    end)
  end

  def report(stock_csv, movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock_csv
    |> anomalies(movement_csv, count_csv)
    |> Enum.map_join("\n", fn anomaly ->
      "sku=#{anomaly.sku} expected=#{anomaly.expected} counted=#{anomaly.counted}"
    end)
  end

  defp parse_pairs(csv) do
    csv
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.drop(1)
    |> Map.new(fn line ->
      [sku, value] = String.split(line, ",", trim: true)
      {sku, String.to_integer(value)}
    end)
  end
end

args.new_string:
defmodule InventoryAudit do
  @moduledoc false

  @add_types ["shipment", "return", "adjustment"]
  @subtract_types ["sale", "damage"]

  def anomalies(stock_csv, movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock = parse_pairs(stock_csv)
    counts = parse_pairs(count_csv)
    movements = parse_movements(movement_csv)

    stock
    |> Enum.map(fn {sku, on_hand} ->
      movement_delta = Map.get(movements, sku, 0)
      expected = on_hand + movement_delta
      counted = Map.get(counts, sku, expected)
      delta = counted - expected

      %{
        sku: sku,
        expected: expected,
        counted: counted,
        delta: delta,
        status: status(delta)
      }
    end)
    |> Enum.filter(fn anomaly -> anomaly.delta != 0 end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(fn anomaly -> {-abs(anomaly.delta), anomaly.sku} end)
  end

  def report(stock_csv, movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock_csv
    |> anomalies(movement_csv, count_csv)
    |> Enum.map_join("\n", fn anomaly ->
      "sku=#{anomaly.sku} expected=#{anomaly.expected} counted=#{anomaly.counted} delta=#{anomaly.delta} status=#{anomaly.status}"
    end)
  end

  defp status(delta) when delta > 0, do: "over"
  defp status(delta) when delta < 0, do: "short"

  defp parse_pairs(csv) do
    csv
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.drop(1)
    |> Map.new(fn line ->
      [sku, value] = String.split(line, ",", trim: true)
      {sku, String.to_integer(value)}
    end)
  end

  defp parse_movements(csv) do
    csv
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.drop(1)
    |> Enum.reduce(%{}, fn line, acc ->
      [type, sku, quantity] = String.split(line, ",", trim: true)
      qty = String.to_integer(quantity)

      delta = cond do
        type in @add_types -> qty
        type in @subtract_types -> -qty
        true -> 0
      end

      Map.update(acc, sku, delta, &(&1 + delta))
    end)
  end
end

cat > /workspace/lib/inventory_audit.ex << 'EOF'
defmodule InventoryAudit do
  @moduledoc false

  @add_types ["shipment", "return", "adjustment"]
  @subtract_types ["sale", "damage"]

  def anomalies(stock_csv, movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock = parse_pairs(stock_csv)
    counts = parse_pairs(count_csv)
    movements = parse_movements(movement_csv)

    stock
    |> Enum.map(fn {sku, on_hand} ->
      movement_delta = Map.get(movements, sku, 0)
      expected = on_hand + movement_delta
      counted = Map.get(counts, sku, expected)
      delta = counted - expected

      %{
        sku: sku,
        expected: expected,
        counted: counted,
        delta: delta,
        status: status(delta)
      }
    end)
    |> Enum.filter(fn anomaly -> anomaly.delta != 0 end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(fn anomaly -> {-abs(anomaly.delta), anomaly.sku} end)
  end

  def report(stock_csv, movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock_csv
    |> anomalies(movement_csv, count_csv)
    |> Enum.map_join("\n", fn anomaly ->
      "sku=#{anomaly.sku} expected=#{anomaly.expected} counted=#{anomaly.counted} delta=#{anomaly.delta} status=#{anomaly.status}"
    end)
  end

  defp status(delta) when delta > 0, do: "over"
  defp status(delta) when delta < 0, do: "short"

  defp parse_pairs(csv) do
    csv
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.drop(1)
    |> Map.new(fn line ->
      [sku, value] = String.split(line, ",", trim: true)
      {sku, String.to_integer(value)}
    end)
  end

  defp parse_movements(csv) do
    csv
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.drop(1)
    |> Enum.reduce(%{}, fn line, acc ->
      [type, sku, quantity] = String.split(line, ",", trim: true)
      qty = String.to_integer(quantity)

      delta = cond do
        type in @add_types -> qty
        type in @subtract_types -> -qty
        true -> 0
      end

      Map.update(acc, sku, delta, &(&1 + delta))
    end)
  end
end
EOF

cat > /workspace/lib/inventory_audit.ex << 'EOF'
defmodule InventoryAudit do
  @moduledoc false

  @add_types ["shipment", "return", "adjustment"]
  @subtract_types ["sale", "damage"]

  def anomalies(stock_csv, movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock = parse_pairs(stock_csv)
    counts = parse_pairs(count_csv)
    movements = parse_movements(movement_csv)

    stock
    |> Enum.map(fn {sku, on_hand} ->
      movement_delta = Map.get(movements, sku, 0)
      expected = on_hand + movement_delta
      counted = Map.get(counts, sku, expected)
      delta = counted - expected

      %{
        sku: sku,
        expected: expected,
        counted: counted,
        delta: delta,
        status: status(delta)
      }
    end)
    |> Enum.filter(fn anomaly -> anomaly.delta != 0 end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(fn anomaly -> {-abs(anomaly.delta), anomaly.sku} end)
  end

  def report(stock_csv, movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock_csv
    |> anomalies(movement_csv, count_csv)
    |> Enum.map_join("\n", fn anomaly ->
      "sku=#{anomaly.sku} expected=#{anomaly.expected} counted=#{anomaly.counted} delta=#{anomaly.delta} status=#{anomaly.status}"
    end)
  end

  defp status(delta) when delta > 0, do: "over"
  defp status(delta) when delta < 0, do: "short"
  defp status(_delta), do: "ok"

  defp parse_pairs(csv) do
    csv
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.drop(1)
    |> Map.new(fn line ->
      [sku, value] = String.split(line, ",", trim: true)
      {sku, String.to_integer(value)}
    end)
  end

  defp parse_movements(csv) do
    csv
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.drop(1)
    |> Enum.reduce(%{}, fn line, acc ->
      [type, sku, quantity] = String.split(line, ",", trim: true)
      qty = String.to_integer(quantity)

      delta = cond do
        type in @add_types -> qty
        type in @subtract_types -> -qty
        true -> 0
      end

      Map.update(acc, sku, delta, &(&1 + delta))
    end)
  end
end
EOF

args.report:
Fixed the InventoryAudit reconciler by implementing the following changes:

**Files Changed:**
- `/workspace/lib/inventory_audit.ex`

**Issues Fixed:**
1. Added movement processing with `parse_movements/1` function
2. Applied movement rules: `shipment`, `return`, `adjustment` add quantity; `sale`, `damage` subtract quantity
3. Added filtering to exclude zero deltas (only real anomalies reported)
4. Added proper sorting: by absolute delta descending, then by SKU ascending
5. Fixed status logic: "over" for positive deltas, "short" for negative
6. Added missing `delta` and `status` fields to report output format

**Commands Run:**
```bash
cd /workspace && mix test
```

**Result:** 2 tests, 0 failures - Acceptance check passed.
args.summary: Repaired InventoryAudit module to parse CSV inputs, apply all movement rules (shipment/return/adjustment add; sale/damage subtract), filter non-zero deltas, sort by absolute delta desc then SKU asc, and format the report correctly. All tests pass.
